content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 2, 1990

Carolyn G. Ortiz
Executive Director
Prison Industry Board
P.O. Box 942883
Sacramento, CA 94283-0001

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of
Telephone Advice
Our File No. A-90-185

Dear Ms. Ortiz:

This is in response to your request for confirmation of
telephone advice on behalf of Mr. Wayne A. Zitzke, member of the
Prison Industry Board, regarding his duties under the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").¹ The Prison Industry Board sets policy
for and oversees the operation of the Prison Industry Authority.

Your letter correctly summarizes the telephone advice given
to you, except for that portion of the advice dealing with
Electrical Supply of Salinas' formulating another contract with
the Prison Industry Authority after Mr. Zitzke resigns from the
Prison Industry Board.²

Section 87401 provides:

No former state administrative official, after
the termination of his or her employment or term of

_________________________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Please note that the telephone advice, which is conservative in
nature, focused on Mr. Zitzke's apparent involvement in the
proceedings as discussed in our telephone conversation. It is
possible that upon further detailed analysis the facts might
indicate that Mr. Zitzke did not "participate" in those
proceedings within the meaning of Sections 87400-87402.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Our File No. A-90-185
Page 2

office, shall for compensation act as agent or
attorney for, or otherwise represent, any other
person (other than the State of California) before
any court or state administrative agency or any
officer or employee thereof by making any formal or
informal appearance, or by making any oral or
written communication with the intent to influence,
in connection with any judicial, quasi-judicial or
other proceeding if both of the following apply:

(a) The State of California is a party or has
a direct and substantial interest.

(b) The proceeding is one in which the former
state administrative official participated.

Section 87402 provides:

No former state administrative official, after
the termination of his or her employment or term of
office shall for compensation aid, advise, counsel,
consult or assist in representing any other person
(except the State of California) in any proceeding
in which the official would be prohibited from
appearing under Section 87401.

Thus, the prohibition applies only to contracts and other
proceedings in which Mr. Zitzke participated during his tenure on
the Prison Industry Board. Therefore, the prohibition would not
apply to new contracts which Electrical Supply of Salinas would
attempt to formulate with the Prison Industry Authority after
Mr. Zitzke resigns from the Prison Industry Board.

I trust this letter provides you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this
matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Jeevan S. Ahuja
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JSA:aa

sections.conclusion: Thus, the prohibition applies only to contracts and other proceedings in which Mr. Zitzke participated during his tenure on the Prison Industry Board. Therefore, the prohibition would not apply to new contracts which Electrical Supply of Salinas would attempt to formulate with the Prison Industry Authority after Mr. Zitzke resigns from the Prison Industry Board.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether former state administrative official Wayne A. Zitzke, upon resigning from the Prison Industry Board, is prohibited under the Political Reform Act from representing Electrical Supply of Salinas in formulating new contracts with the Prison Industry Authority.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The restrictions in Sections 87401 and 87402 of the Political Reform Act apply only to proceedings in which the former official participated during their tenure. Therefore, Mr. Zitzke would not be prohibited from representing Electrical Supply of Salinas in new contracts formulated after his resignation.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains no clearly marked QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections; sections extracted from body text. Footnote 2 indicates this is confirmation of prior telephone advice. OCR quality issues present in document but key content readable.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether former state administrative official Wayne A. Zitzke, upon resigning from the Prison Industry Board, is prohibited under the Political Reform Act from representing Electrical Supply of Salinas in formulating new contracts with the Prison Industry Authority.

CONCLUSION: Thus, the prohibition applies only to contracts and other proceedings in which Mr. Zitzke participated during his tenure on the Prison Industry Board. Therefore, the prohibition would not apply to new contracts which Electrical Supply of Salinas would attempt to formulate with the Prison Industry Authority after Mr. Zitzke resigns from the Prison Industry Board.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice regarding Wayne A. Zitzke's post-employment restrictions under the Political Reform Act, clarifying that former state administrative officials are only prohibited from representing private parties in proceedings they participated in during their tenure, not in new future proceedings.
